bunking.def: 睡在臥舖、共同使用的人 (或物) (舖)
bunking.ex: The soldiers spent the night bunking in the barracks.

bunkum.ex: His political speech was nothing but total bunkum.

bunny.def: 小兔子、(俚)女人的人、可愛的人物、小兔地 (兔)
bunny.col: easter bunny, beach bunny, dust bunny (積在地上的毛屑)
bunny.ex: The child carried a soft stuffed bunny to bed.

bunting.def: 國旗布、裝飾用的小旗、鵐(鳥)的人 (或物) (旗)
bunting.ex: The streets were decorated with colorful festive bunting.

buoy.def: 浮標、使浮起、支撐、救生圈的人、浮起地 (浮標)
buoy.col: life buoy, channel buoy, buoy up (使振奮)
buoy.ex: The bright yellow buoy marks the edge of the channel.

buoyancy.def: 浮力、輕快、樂觀、能浮的人 (或物) (浮力)
buoyancy.ex: The boat's design provides excellent water buoyancy.

buoyant.def: 有浮力的、輕快的、上漲的、樂觀的的人 (或物)
buoyant.ex: He has a buoyant personality and never seems sad.

buoyantly.def: 有浮力地、輕快地、樂觀地
buoyantly.ex: They chatted buoyantly about their upcoming holiday.

bur/burr.def: (植物)刺果、(鑽)毛刺、去除毛刺、刺果的人、刺果地 (刺)
bur/burr.ex: A rough burr was stuck to the dog's thick coat.

burden.def: 擔子、負擔、重任、負重、使負擔、重任的人物、負重地 (重)
burden.col: be a burden to, bear the burden, financial burden, burden of proof
burden.ex: The national debt is a heavy burden on the people.

burdensome.ex: The new regulations proved to be very burdensome.

bureau.col: FB I (聯邦調查局), travel bureau, news bureau
bureau.ex: He works for the state's intelligence bureau.

bureaucracy.def: 官僚政治、繁文縟節、官僚 (總稱) (單數)
bureaucracy.ex: The project was delayed by unnecessary bureaucracy.

bureaucrat.ex: The citizen felt ignored by the faceless bureaucrat.

bureaucratic.ex: We faced many bureaucratic hurdles at the local hall.

burgeon.def: 萌芽、急速成長、嫩芽的人、萌芽地 (萌)
burgeon.ex: The small town burgeoned into a major metropolis.

burgeoning.def: 生機勃勃的、飛速發展的的人 (或物) (勃)
burgeoning.ex: The company is meeting the needs of a burgeoning market.

burger.ex: He ordered a juicy cheeseburger with extra lettuce.

burgher.ex: The wealthy burghers controlled the city's trade.

burglar.ex: The convicted burglar was sentenced to four years.

burglarize.def: (美)破門盜竊的人 (或物)、盜竊地 (盜)
burglarize.ex: The house was burglarized while they were on vacation.

burglarproof.ex: He installed burglarproof locks on all the doors.

burglary.def: 盜竊罪、破門盜竊行為 (單數)
burglary.ex: Burglary rates have decreased in the city center.

burgle.ex: Their office was burgled during the weekend.

burgomaster.ex: The ancient burgomaster presided over the town council.

burgundy.def: 勃艮第葡萄酒、深紅色、深紅色的的人物、深紅地 (紅)
burgundy.ex: She wore a stunning dress in rich burgundy silk.

burial.def: 埋葬、葬禮、埋葬的、埋葬的人 (或物)、埋葬地 (葬)
burial.ex: The ancient burial site was discovered by archeologists.

buried.def: (bury的過去式及過去分詞)、隱藏的
buried.ex: Legends tell of buried treasure on the lonely island.

burlesque.def: 滑稽模仿、滑稽戲、模仿的人物、滑稽地 (仿)
burlesque.ex: The show is a brilliant burlesque of modern office life.

burly.def: 魁梧的、強壯的、結實的人物
burly.ex: He was a burly man with broad shoulders.
